---
title: "Elly Kirwa"
subtitle: "Graduate Research Assistant | PhD Student | Epidemiology & Applied Data Science"
image: misc/info/photo.png

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/elly-kirwa-04a351123/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/EllyKp

listing:
- id: blog
  contents:
    - "../blog/*/index.qmd"
    - "../blog/*/index.markdown"
  sort: date desc
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 1
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [title, description, author, date, image]
  max-items: 1
  image-height: "200"
- id: talk
  contents:
    - "../talks/*/index.qmd"
  sort: date desc
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 1
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [title, author, date, image]
  max-items: 1
  image-height: "200"

- id: project
  contents:
    - "../projects/*.qmd"
    - "../projects/*/index.markdown"
  sort: date desc
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 1
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [title, author, date, image]
  max-items: 1
  image-height: "200"

- id: publication
  contents:
    - "../publications/articles/*.qmd"
    - "../publications/*/index.markdown"
  sort: date desc
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 1
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [title, author, date, image]
  max-items: 1
  image-height: "200"

format:
  html:
    linestretch: 1.9
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    page-tools: false
---

::: {#hero-heading}
Hi! I'm <strong style="color:#007bff;">Elly Kirwa</strong>, a Doctoral Researcher in Epidemiology and Population Animal Sciences and an applied data scientist, with a strong focus on integrating statistical modeling, machine learning, and economic analysis to improve decision-making in commercial livestock systems.

My current PhD research centers on optimizing herd efficiency by modeling sow- and parity-specific performance trajectories to identify predictors of reproductive performance, lifetime productivity, retention, and economic return under commercial production conditions. By reframing routinely collected herd data as longitudinal performance signals, I develop analytics that help producers distinguish sustainable productivity from short-term biological or economic gains.

**Current Research Focus**

I develop and validate integrated analytics pipelines that combine biological performance with economic outcomes, including:

- Predictive modeling of sow reproductive outcomes, longevity, and retention
- Sentinel surveillance and anomaly detection using reproductive and performance indicators
- Economic analyses of reproductive failure, sow removal, and replacement decisions
- Simulation and scenario analyses to evaluate management strategies under varying economic conditions
- Explainable machine learning to support transparent, decision-focused interpretation

My work emphasizes biological plausibility, economic relevance, and operational feasibility, ensuring that analytical outputs translate directly into barn-level and business-level decisions.

In parallel with my PhD, I am completing a Graduate Certificate in Business Analytics, strengthening my training in data-driven decision-making, optimization, forecasting, and economic modeling. My background spans veterinary medicine, pharmacology, toxicology, and preventive medicine, with early research focused on antimicrobial resistance, milk safety, and production data analysis. This multidisciplinary foundation allows me to bridge animal health, analytics, and economics in applied production environments.

**Key Strengths & Contributions**

- **Applied Analytics for Production & Economics** — Development of predictive and economic benchmarking tools that inform sow retention, reproductive management, replacement strategies, and cost-effective herd optimization.
- **Field-Based & Industry-Oriented Perspective** — Experience working with real-world commercial data to address biological and economic trade-offs faced by producers and production systems.
- **Advanced Modeling & Business Analytics** — Expertise in machine learning, mixed-effects and survival models, economic simulations, partial budgeting, and scenario-based optimization.
- **Decision-Support & Visualization** — Translation of complex biological and economic outputs into intuitive dashboards and tools for producers, veterinarians, and decision-makers.
- **Scientific & Stakeholder Communication** — Strong experience in manuscript development, grant writing, and cross-sector collaboration with academic and industry partners.

:::
